rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute chest-wall pain and breathlessness after prolonged tight chest binding, with depersonalisation, in a patient who is haemodynamically stable but whose chest cannot be examined remotely. Spontaneous pneumothorax and rib injury cannot be excluded via telehealth (no auscultation, palpation or imaging), and the source note itself required urgent out-of-hours face-to-face assessment to exclude respiratory compromise/pneumothorax. The telehealth-correct action is to validate the symptom and escalate to in-person urgent assessment today (T4 / ED or urgent care) — not 000 (the patient is stable), but never routine or 'reassure it's stress', which would be dismissive under-triage. A parallel mandatory mental-health safety screen applies.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden severe or one-sided (tearing/stabbing) chest pain, rapidly worsening breathlessness, or coughing up blood
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests pneumothorax (possibly tension) — an immediate threat to life needing emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain suddenly gets much worse or moves to one side, if your breathing gets rapidly worse, or if you cough up blood — call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Lips or fingertips turning blue, inability to catch breath, or collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: Signs of severe hypoxia or respiratory failure — emergency ambulance and resuscitation capability required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your lips or fingers go blue, you can't catch your breath, or you feel like you might pass out — call 000 right away and stay with your housemate.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Active thoughts of self-harm or of ending your life, or a plan
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 or a crisis line now (e.g. Lifeline 13 11 14) and do not stay alone
escalation_edges.rationale: Body-image/gender distress with restrictive eating carries real self-harm risk; active ideation is a crisis requiring immediate escalation and not being left alone.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you have thoughts of harming yourself or that life isn't worth living, please call 000 or Lifeline on 13 11 14 now, and stay with your housemate — you don't have to face this alone.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest pain or breathlessness not improving after removing the binder, or unable to get an in-person assessment today
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend an emergency department the same day
escalation_edges.rationale: Persisting symptoms after removing the mechanical cause need in-person examination and possible imaging to exclude pneumothorax/rib injury.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your chest or breathing doesn't ease after taking the binder off, or you can't get seen in person today, go to your nearest emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Gender-diverse patient with likely prior experiences of medical dismissal; discloses body/identity distress reluctantly
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use affirming, non-judgmental language; explicitly validate that the symptoms are real and worth taking seriously. Dismissive or invalidating framing risks the patient disengaging and not attending the in-person assessment — which is the exact harm this case tests for.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Restrictive eating with low body weight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Flag malnutrition risk to the in-person clinician; take any NSAID with food; incorporate nutritional and psychological support in follow-up.
Enlist the housemate to help arrange transport to in-person assessment and to stay with the patient; a supportive person present is protective for the mental-health risk.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Take the tight binder off now to relieve the pressure on your chest. Your chest pain and breathlessness are real, and because a clinician can't examine your chest over the phone, you need to be seen in person today so they can check your chest and rule out a collapsed lung or a rib injury. Try slow, gentle breathing while you arrange it, and have your housemate stay with you. Call 000 straight away if your breathing suddenly gets worse, the pain becomes sudden or one-sided, you cough up blood, your lips go blue, or you have any thoughts of harming yourself.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend an emergency department or urgent care today, or phone your GP for a same-day in-person appointment; call 000 for any of the emergency signs above.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it's after hours, go to the nearest emergency department or call the after-hours GP service (13 SICK / healthdirect 1800 022 222). 000 is available 24/7 for emergencies, and Lifeline (13 11 14) for crisis support.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Chest-wall pain and breathlessness related to prolonged chest binding; depersonalisation; restrictive eating with low weight; low mood tied to body/identity. Requests: in-person exclusion of pneumothorax/rib injury, affirming psychological support and gender-affirming referral, dietetic review, and safe-binding advice.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing the chest pain and breathlessness as psychosocial ('just anxiety / stress / body image') and routing to self-care or routine/prompt GP only, without escalating to exclude pneumothorax/rib injury in person.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A missed spontaneous or tension pneumothorax progresses to respiratory collapse; the patient's justified distrust of medical care deepens after another dismissal (medical gaslighting).

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling a 000 ambulance for a haemodynamically stable patient when same-day in-person assessment would suffice. This is a mild error and is preferred to dismissal, because under-triage is weighted 3x over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot auscultate, palpate or image the chest, so spontaneous pneumothorax and rib injury cannot be excluded; the psychosocial driver is evident but must not be used to explain away the unexcluded organic mimic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Name the uncertainty openly, validate the symptom as real, and escalate to in-person urgent assessment while giving clear return-precautions.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Premature psychosocial closure — attributing everything to body-image/gender distress or anxiety and reassuring or deferring to routine follow-up.
